Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve critical Garmin compatibility bugs and implement missing data parsers

- Fix FIT file timestamps: subtract FIT_EPOCH_OFFSET (631065600s) from all
  Unix timestamps so activities import with correct dates (was showing ~2039)
- Fix TCX LX extension: remove invalid HeartRateZone elements (not in
  ActivityExtensionv2.xsd); replace with valid AvgSpeed (km/h→m/s) and Steps
- Fix TCX element ordering: Notes now placed after Lap per schema (Id→Lap→Notes→Creator)
- Remove fabricated heart rate data: eliminate random.randint() variation from
  all synthetic trackpoints in TCX and FIT generators
- Remove MaximumSpeed from TCX: was set to average speed in wrong units (km/h)
- Fix GPS timestamp format: use strftime with Z suffix instead of isoformat()
- Implement _parse_stress_data(): reads Fitbit Stress Journal CSV files
- Implement _parse_temperature_data(): reads nightly skin temp deviation (CSV+JSON)
- Implement _parse_spo2_data(): reads SpO2 CSV+JSON, filters invalid marker (50.0)
- Fix sleep CSV export: add all parsed stage columns (REM, light, deep, wake,
  sleep score, awakening count, breathing rate, avg/min HR, skin temp deviation)
- Fix body composition CSV: clarify weight unit label (lbs vs kg ambiguity)
- Add garmin_body_composition_import.csv: Garmin Connect importable format
  with "Body" header line, weight converted lbs→kg via heuristic (>100 = lbs)
- Add _export_stress_csv(), _export_temperature_csv(), _export_spo2_csv()
  and wire them into export_all_data()
- Update CLAUDE.md with accurate architecture and performance feature notes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SQLite schema: normalize xpaths table, add container-level instance tracking

- xpaths table now stores base xpaths only (~thousands of rows instead of
  millions). Instance numbers like [2], [3] are no longer baked into xpath
  strings.
- fields table gains instance column tracking which repeating group instance
  each field belongs to. Instance numbers are assigned at the container
  level (e.g. which RecipientTable element), so all child fields within the
  same group share the same instance number.
- Fixes misalignment bug where leaf-level instance counting could pair
  fields from different group instances when some fields were missing.
- Query speed for xpath searches: 22s -> 0.015s on 5K filing test.
- fields_view updated to include instance column.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve clipboard paste bug with empty columns causing data shift

- When doing Cmd+A → Cmd+V (full grid paste), the column expansion logic
  used `Column ${N}` naming which could create duplicate column names if
  the target already had a column with that generated name
- Duplicate column names caused both entries to share the same data key,
  so later writes overwrote earlier writes, losing the first data column
- Fix: for full grid paste (selectAll + paste at origin), reset the column
  order to match paste data width exactly — trim excess, add unique names
- Also switched non-full paste expansion to use generateUniqueColumnName
  to prevent duplicates in partial paste scenarios

Fix lesson-31 mobile layout (invalid CSS + input overflow); metallic shimmer all months

  the rule, causing cascading layout failures)

catalogue:

  just given dark-base -> mid -> bright-highlight -> mid -> dark-base structure):
  Jan(violet), Mar(teal), Apr(blue), May(lime), Jun(orange), Jul(red), Aug(cyan),
  Oct(red-orange), Nov(indigo) - Feb/Sep/Dec already had shimmer from previous fix
- Each month uses a slightly different gradient angle for natural variety

commit.message:
Fix: sanitize publishable key — strip secret key leaked in env var

The NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY env var on Vercel contained both
the publishable key and CLERK_SECRET_KEY concatenated (newline-separated).
This caused two issues:
1. Clerk rejected the key as invalid
2. The secret key was leaked to the browser

Now we split on newlines and only use the first line (the pk_* value).

commit.message:
Fix insta-death and redesign stair system with 3 columns

1. Fire now starts at Y=-300 (was -50) giving plenty of breathing room
2. Stairs now use 3 columns (left, center, right) like classic stair
   climbing games. Each stair is randomly placed in a different column
   than the previous one. Player taps left/right relative to their
   current column position.
3. Stair generation uses StairInfo struct tracking column position
4. Direction check is relative (next stair left/right of current)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: content examples + category-specific guidelines for bol-content skill

Enable AI skill training through few-shot learning and category-aware prompts.

## Phase 1: Content Examples System

**Database (Migration 026):**
- Add content_examples table (generic, reusable for Bol + Amazon)
- Supports marketplace, category_slug, example_type, language, rating
- Tracks usage_count for effectiveness analysis

**Seed data:**
- 16 examples across 3 categories (sportlegging, sport-bhs, laptops)
- 2 good + 2 bad titles per category
- 1 good + 1 bad description example per category
- Focus on demonstrating category differences (fashion vs. electronics)

**Prompt Builder:**
- Add fetchCategoryExamples() to fetch category-specific examples
- Inject examples section in buildDatabasePrompt() before rules
- Normalizes category slugs (lowercase + hyphen replacement)
- Graceful fallback if no examples found (no crash)

## Phase 2: Category-Specific Guidelines

**Database (Migration 027):**
- Extend bol_category_attribute_requirements with:
  - content_focus_areas[] (e.g., kleur/maat for fashion, processor/RAM for electronics)
  - tone_guidelines (category-specific tone of voice)
  - priority_usps[] (required USPs to mention)
  - attribute_templates (JSONB templates for phrasing attributes)

**Seed data:**
- Sportlegging: focus on kleur/maat/pasvorm/materiaal, active tone
- Sport-BHS: focus on support level/pasvorm/comfort
- Laptops: focus on processor/RAM/opslag/garantie, technical but accessible tone

**Prompt Builder:**
- Inject category guidelines section after examples
- Shows focus areas, tone guidelines, priority USPs, attribute templates
- Only injects if content_focus_areas is non-empty (backwards compatible)

## Benefits

- Few-shot learning improves content quality without fine-tuning
- Category-aware prompts: fashion emphasizes fit/color, electronics emphasizes specs
- Generic table design supports future Amazon expansion
- Graceful degradation: works without migrations (empty examples, no guidelines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix signature matching: MINOR_VERSION in all sdef=0 lookups

Four bugs caused template matching to never run and use wrong data:

1. sd > 0 guard in matchSignatureAgainstPage blocked ALL template scoring —
   signatures always live at STRUCTURE_DEF_ID=0, so sd was always 0 and
   the condition was always false. Removed the guard entirely.

2. g_line_templates keyed by (sdef, line_id) — at sdef=0 the same LINE_ID
   carries a different TEMPLATE per MINOR_VERSION (one per signature species
   variant). Changed key to (sdef, minor_version, line_id) and updated
   loadLineTemplates to SELECT MINOR_VERSION.

3. g_field_coords keyed by (sdef, line_id, field_id) — same problem: at
   sdef=0 column positions differ per MINOR_VERSION. Changed key to
   (sdef, minor_version, line_id, field_id) and updated loadFieldCoordinates
   to SELECT MINOR_VERSION.

4. loadLinesInSign and loadSensitiveFields joined using MAX(MINOR_VERSION)
   per SIGN_ID globally — wrong because the same SIGN_ID has up to 42
   species-specific versions each with their own MINOR_VERSION and different
   LINE/FIELD sets. Fixed both to join against SIGNATURE's own MINOR_VERSION
   for the active domain and keyed their internal maps by (sign_id, minor_version).

resolveFieldCoords and collectFieldCoords updated accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs(standards): add ROLE-012/013/014 — install quality, error visibility, test coverage

Derived from hostctl role review. Three new requirements cover patterns that
are not yet enforced in the existing spec:

- ROLE-012: Install & Download Quality — no dead install methods, one task per
  operation, trim URL vars, binary verified via command -v, split over regex,
  API headers checked against current docs
- ROLE-013: Error Visibility — failed_when:false requires visible fallback;
  command/shell tasks must declare changed_when and failed_when explicitly
- ROLE-014: Test Coverage Completeness — no hardcoded versions in verify.yml,
  edge cases (empty inputs, all states), idempotence in every test_sequence,
  network tasks exercised in at least one scenario

commit.message:
fix(security): address high-severity CodeQL code scanning alerts (#2197)

* fix(security): fix incomplete string escaping in parseLLMError

The Python repr unescape step used sequential replaces which corrupt
each other (e.g. escaping \ first breaks the \' → ' replacement). Also,
a bare backslash in the message not followed by a recognised escape
character would produce invalid JSON and throw.

Fix: unescape Python repr escapes (\\ → \, \' → ', \" → ") in a
single regex pass before JSON-encoding, so JSON.parse always receives a
well-formed string.

Addresses CodeQL "incomplete-string-escaping-or-encoding" (alert #52).

The three remaining high-severity alerts are false positives:
- #51 onboarding/project.tsx XSS: router.push with getSafeReturnToPath-
  validated path; Next.js router does not interpret paths as HTML.
- #48 addEnvs.ts password hash: SHA-256 generates a cache key from
  projectId+salt+month, not a password. authenticateApiKey taint is
  a false CodeQL flow.
- #42 [project]/index.tsx dynamic call: lookup in trusted server-side
  dependencies object, guarded by existence check — no arbitrary dispatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): anchor Google image host checks to subdomain boundaries

endsWith("gstatic.com") matches evil-gstatic.com, allowing content
from untrusted domains to be proxied as if from Google infrastructure.
Fix uses hostname === "gstatic.com" || endsWith(".gstatic.com") so
only actual subdomains are accepted.

Addresses CodeQL alerts #11 and #18 (incomplete URL substring sanitization).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: fix swapped test descriptions in ExternalImage.test.ts

The lh3.googleusercontent.com test was labelled "gstatic.com" and the
encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com test was labelled "googleusercontent.com".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: comprehensive code review fixes — security, cleanup, and hardening

Security: remove hardcoded admin secret fallbacks, add prompt injection
boundaries (wrap user input in <user_input> tags), add AI proxy model
whitelist, fix sharing route column name (password_hash→password),
reduce global body-parser limit to 2MB (50MB on image routes only),
increase trial password minimum to 8 chars, move FreeImage API key
to env var.

Cleanup: remove duplicate rate limiters and authenticateToken from
server.js (use middleware imports), delete dead files (database/config.js,
server/index.js, temp prompts, test-face-variations), remove stale config
exports, remove replicate dependency, replace ~37 console.log with
log.info, move test scripts to scripts/admin/.

Hardening: fix storyAvatarGeneration logger import path, use req.ip
instead of manual X-Forwarded-For parsing, deduplicate buildStoryMetadata
(import from database.js), fix Stripe userId parseInt→string comparison.

Full review documented in docs/code-review-2026-03-10.md.
